title: Rebase, Sequencer, Autosquash, and Topology Preservation · Repository Trace: Gita Interactive Todo Actions
summary: A stacked change series must move between release bases, preserve selected merges, reorder dependent commits, and satisfy a review policy without losing authorship or test evidence. Planned artifact: Add and verify the gita interactive todo actions increment in rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, exec, drop, break, label, reset, and merge actions while preserving dependency order.
Use --onto, upstream, branch, fork-point, and explicit revision sets to select exactly which commits move and where.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-advanced cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Predict Gita Interactive Todo Actions
steps.instruction:
theory case — Case G60 is a theory in a disposable repository. the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity. The learner must predict range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries, run git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD, explain four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review, test this changed condition: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge, and preserve this recovery boundary: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Gita Interactive Todo Actions contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, exec, drop, break, label, reset, and merge actions while preserving dependency order.

content.text: Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, exec, drop, break, label, reset, and merge actions while preserving dependency order. Use this as an observable contract: name its preconditions, trace the state or value change, and state what evidence would disprove the result.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Locate repository and operation state, compare working tree, index, refs, and graph, preserve a recovery reference, preview the smallest change, then verify both tree behavior and history topology.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Case G60 is a worked example in a disposable repository. the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity. The learner must predict range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries, run git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD, explain four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review, test this changed condition: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge, and preserve this recovery boundary: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass. A defensible solution applies Gita Interactive Todo Actions, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to rebase todo, mapping, and equivalence proof.

content.code:
# Advanced Git 2.55 deterministic scenario 1
# before-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# hypothesis: the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity
git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD
# expected: range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries
# observation: four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review
# changed-case: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge
# after-ref: main=a11ce00 candidate=b00b1e5
# tree-proof: compare the exact affected tree and paths before judging success
# behavior-proof: retain a changed-case test or invariant that can falsify the Git-only conclusion
# recovery: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass
# transfer-limit: coordinate real shared-history rewrites and publish with an explicit lease expectation
content.caption: Runnable repository-state trace example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: Interactive rebase actions may be reordered freely because the final tree is all reviewers need. This fails the Gita Interactive Todo Actions contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.

steps.title: Build Gita Interactive Todo Actions evidence
steps.instruction: Append an Advanced Git evidence block headed "# Evidence: gita-interactive-todo-actions-1s9x2f" with exact before and after refs; a falsifiable prediction; the bounded simulator command; expected, observed, tree, behavior, changed-case, and recovery evidence; and one honest real-repository transfer limit.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Gita Interactive Todo Actions into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: gita-interactive-todo-actions-1s9x2f
# scenario-key: trace-1s9x2f probe-f2x9s1 boundary-9x2f1s owner-1s9x2fx fault-1s9x2fz
# before-ref: main=a11ce1s candidate=b00b12f
# hypothesis: the rewrite preserved patch intent while changing parentage and commit identity
git range-diff refs/recovery/pre-rebase...HEAD
# expected: range-diff maps old and new patches and exposes reordered, amended, dropped, or new entries
# observation: four patches map cleanly and one amended policy patch requires focused review
# changed-case: drop a dependency patch in the scenario and observe the downstream mapping and behavior diverge
# after-ref: main=a11ce1s candidate=c0ffeef
# tree-proof: the affected tree and path set match the predicted repository transition
# behavior-proof: a changed input preserves the named product or release invariant
# recovery: keep refs/recovery/pre-rebase until tree, tests, authorship, and publication review pass
# transfer-limit: coordinate real shared-history rewrites and publish with an explicit lease expectation
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use pick, reword, edit, squash, fixup, exec, drop, break, label, reset, and merge actions while preserving dependency order.
